alerts.title: Iran confirms Khamenei wounded in prior US-Israel strike
alerts.summary: An Iranian health official publicly stated Supreme Leader Khamenei sustained 'superficial' wounds during the recent US-Israel strike campaign (reported by Breakingthenews, Straits Times, Vanguard). The first official acknowledgment that a strike physically reached Khamenei marks a major disclosure mid-negotiation and reframes Tehran's domestic posture as Khamenei simultaneously hardens uranium red lines.

alerts.title: Iran reconstituting missile capability; Larestan underground base back online
alerts.summary: Times of Israel reports Iran is rebuilding missile production faster than US/Israeli analysts expected, while ME Spectator (~52K views) says the entrance to the Larestan underground missile complex in southern Iran near the Persian Gulf has been repaired and the base is operational again. The reconstitution is occurring concurrently with the 'largely negotiated' Hormuz deal, suggesting Tehran is hedging rather than de-escalating.

alerts.title: Russia reportedly fires two 'Oreshnik' IRBMs at Ukraine in single night
alerts.summary: Rybar (~143K views) reports that during the May 24 strikes on Kyiv, Bila Tserkva and Druzhkovka, Russian forces conducted two 'Oreshnik' intermediate-range ballistic missile launches alongside 'Tsirkon' hypersonics — described as the first dual-Oreshnik employment of the war. DeepState UA separately notes NATO's ARRC rehearsed deep-strike contingencies against Russia from a London bunker under 'Operation Arcade Strike,' indicating both sides are signaling escalation dominance.

alerts.title: GOP hawks revolt against Trump's emerging Iran deal
alerts.summary: Middle East Eye reports Republican hawks are publicly attacking the emerging US-Iran framework as a 'nightmare for Israel,' with US sentiment data showing 'gop,' 'criticism,' 'tillis,' and 'hegseth' all spiking in coverage. NYT separately frames Netanyahu as reduced from 'co-pilot' to 'passenger' in Trump's Iran policy, suggesting the deal faces a domestic political squeeze even before it is signed.

alerts.title: Iran's Tasnim outlines hardline MoU terms; US official sets 'No Dust, No Dollars' rule
alerts.summary: ME Spectator (~45K views) cites Iran's semi-official Tasnim demanding at least half of frozen Iranian assets be released up front, zero nuclear commitments, and a full Lebanon ceasefire with no breaches. A senior US official told Fox News no agreement will be signed 'today or tomorrow,' citing a 'No Dust, No Dollars' policy — confirming the two sides remain far apart on sequencing despite Trump's 'largely negotiated' framing.

analysis_note: US-Iran talks are publicly converging on a Hormuz/sanctions framework while both sides quietly harden: Iran has disclosed Khamenei was wounded, is rebuilding missile infrastructure, and is demanding upfront asset releases, while Trump faces a GOP hawk revolt — all against a Threat Level 9 backdrop with Brent at $100 and Russia debuting dual-Oreshnik strikes on Ukraine.
